1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CIRCULATION_MOC_MEDSEA_area_averaged_mean 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
Time mean meridional Eulerian streamfunctions  are computed using the velocity field 
estimate provided by the Copernicus Marine Mediterranean Sea reanalysis from 1987 
to the year preceding the current one [-1Y] (including both Reanalysis and Interim 
timeseries), operationally extended  yearly. The Eule rian meridional streamfunction is 
evaluated by integrating meridional velocity daily data first in a vertical direction, 
then in a meridional direction, and finally averaging over the reanalysis period:  
 
Where: H refers to the bathymetry, x B1 and xB2 define the zonal boundaries, and T = t 1–
t0 represents the time interval of interest (Pinardi et al. 2019).  
The Mediterranean overturning indices are derived for the eastern and western 
Mediterranean Sea by computing the annual streamfunction in the two areas 
separated by the Strait of Sicily aroun d 36.5°N, and then considering the associated 
maxima.  
 
 
In each case a geographical constraint focused the computation on the main region 
of interest. For the western index, we focused in deep -water formation regions, thus 
excluding both the effect of shallow physical processes and the Gibraltar net inflow. 
For the eastern index, we investigate the Levantine and Cretan areas corresponding 
to the strongest meridional overturning cell locations, thus only a zonal constraint is 
defined.

2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Mediterranean Meridional Overturning Circulation Index 
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the Copernicus Marine 
Ocean State Report issue 4 (OSR4, von Schuckmann et al., 2020) Section 2.4 
(Lyubartsev et al., 2020). With respect to t he results shown in OSR4, an updated higher 
resolution Mediterranean

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document describes the evaluation method and scientific validation of OMI Med Overturning Circulation, focusing on the computation of time mean meridional Eulerian streamfunctions using velocity field estimates from Copernicus Marine Mediterranean Sea reanalysis.
